era_context: The Roman Empire under Antoninus Pius is in the mid-phase of his reign (138–161 CE). The Eastern Han Dynasty under Emperor Huan continues its decline. The Kushan Empire is at its height. This is a period of relative stability in the Mediterranean world, though the Antonine Plague will emerge within a decade.

events.title: Antoninus Pius reigns; no major recorded events
events.description: The year 156 CE falls within the reign of Antoninus Pius (138–161 CE), a period of relative peace and stability in the Roman Empire. No major military campaigns, natural disasters, or political crises are recorded for this specific year. The emperor maintained the status quo, focusing on administrative consolidation and frontier defense. This was one of the most peaceful periods in Roman imperial history.
events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for 156 CE. The characterization is based on the broader pattern of Antoninus Pius's reign.

events.title: Eastern Han Dynasty: Eunuch dominance and factional conflict
events.description: Emperor Huan's reign (146–168 CE) continued to be characterized by eunuch dominance and the suppression of scholar-official factions. The year 156 CE saw no recorded major events, but the structural decay of the Han state persisted. The balance of power had shifted decisively toward the imperial harem and eunuchs, weakening the Confucian bureaucracy and central authority.

sources.name: Hou Han Shu (History of the Later Han)

events.certainty_note: No specific events are recorded for 156 CE.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The absence of recorded events reflects the bias of surviving sources toward recording crises rather than routine administration.
historiographic_note: Documentation for 156 CE is sparse. The surviving sources provide minimal detail for individual years during the reign of Antoninus Pius. The bias toward recording crises and major events means that years of relative stability are underrepresented in the historical record. For the Eastern Han, sources are similarly sparse for individual years during this period.

graph_edges.note: Antoninus Pius's reign ends in 161 CE
